scope: PowerShell engine and Pester evidence boundary for the #260 PowerShell validation series. This artifact defines evidence ownership only; workflow YAML, PR, SARIF upload, required-check policy, and external-agent invocation are out of scope.

engine_matrix.boundary: Discovers tracked PowerShell surfaces. It does not prove Windows PowerShell 5.1 runtime compatibility or analyzer quality.

engine_matrix.output_artifact: workflow or local Windows PowerShell 5.1 validation report
engine_matrix.boundary: Proves parser/runtime compatibility only for the checked surfaces. It does not prove PSScriptAnalyzer enforcement.
Analyzer signal may run under PowerShell 7 when module availability requires it. It must be reported separately from Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatibility evidence.
Proves selected analyzer/custom-check risk mappings catch realistic weak-check cases. It is independent of Pester.
DCOIR-specific static checks cover known review risks that generic analyzer rules do not catch. They are independent of Pester.

engine_matrix.required_engine: python-3.12; optional PowerShell 7 Core parser when available
engine_matrix.runner_os: any for deterministic parity, windows-latest when Windows parser evidence is required
engine_matrix.boundary: Proves source parts and generated outputs remain mapped and fresh. Windows PowerShell 5.1 runtime compatibility remains separate evidence.
Classifies analyzer/custom findings and baseline/suppression deltas. It does not replace engine-specific validation.

engine_matrix.required_engine: PowerShell 7 Core or Windows PowerShell 5.1 Desktop as declared by the owning test
engine_matrix.runner_os: windows-latest when Windows PowerShell 5.1 behavior is asserted; otherwise any runner with declared engine
engine_matrix.module_or_tool_dependency: Pester with pinned or reported version
engine_matrix.output_artifact: future Pester test result artifact when a later gate enables it
engine_matrix.owner: #267 boundary only until a later Pester/runtime-test gate approves execution
engine_matrix.boundary: Pester may support wrapper, fixture, and runtime-test evidence, but it is not a substitute for analyzer/custom-check enforcement.

may_validate: runtime tests owned by an explicit future gate

owned_responsibilities.notes: May prove wrapper call behavior but must not be the only analyzer enforcement proof.
May add Pester test output around existing fixture expectations.

owned_responsibilities.owner: existing collector runtime validation lanes or future explicit runtime/Pester issue
owned_responsibilities.notes: Runtime compatibility remains separate from static/security validation.

required_conditions: rule-risk fixture report succeeds with negative fixture findings
custom check report succeeds with custom findings
finding governance report classifies all analyzer/custom findings
Pester is not required for these enforcement proofs
